decisions.summary: Instrument First, Memory Second, Retention Third — a tightly sequenced three-phase execution where no phase begins until the prior phase...
decisions.context: Reduce Day-1 through Day-7 abandonment from ~75% to below 40% by redesigning the onboarding and first-session experience with persistent AI memory, behavioral retention mechanics, and a personalization loop that delivers perceived value before the user has a reason to quit.
decisions.why_this_won: Instrument First, Memory Second, Retention Third — a tightly sequenced three-phase execution where no phase begins until the prior phase delivers a decision-forcing output. Phase 1 (Days 1–7): Install lightweight session event logging to identify exact abandonment coordinates within the Day-1 funnel — this eliminates the single most dangerous assumption in the entire problem frame. Phase 2 (Days 8–21): Ship the minimum viable persistent memory layer using structured Supabase key-value storage before any vector approach, bounded by the $200/month constraint — memory must be live before behavioral mechanics are layered on top or they operate on a broken substrate. Phase 3 (Days 22–30): Design and deploy one commitment device and one variable reward mechanic directly informed by the Phase 1 drop-off data and Phase 2 memory architecture — not before. Onboarding redesign runs in parallel with Phase 2 using Phase 1 findings as the brief. Cold-start personalization is scoped as a Phase 2 output, not a separate workstream. This path is chosen over parallel execution because solo founder bandwidth is the binding constraint — sequencing forces focus and prevents the three-front execution failure that kills bootstrapped products.
risks_accepted: Assumption invalidation risk — the current working assumption that abandonment occurs between workout generation and workout completion may be wrong; if drop-off is at signup or intake, the entire onboarding redesign brief changes materially — instrumentation must run before any redesign work begins

Memory layer cost blowout — injecting user history as context tokens into every coaching session is the single most likely path to breaching the $200/month ceiling; structured key-value memory with selective retrieval must be the default architecture, not full-history injection

Solo founder context-switching overhead — each workstream transition carries a cognitive switching cost that compounds across a 30-day sprint; Michael must time-box each phase with hard exits, not soft transitions

Behavioral mechanics misfiring on high-intent users — loss aversion nudges and streak mechanics calibrated for low-engagement consumer apps may feel patronizing or anxiety-inducing to motivated fitness users who abandoned because of perceived value failure, not motivation failure; freya must segment mechanic design by abandonment reason, not apply a uniform behavioral layer

Cold-start personalization overpromising — if the first-session workout recommendation is marketed as AI-personalized but feels generic due to cold-start limitations, the trust deficit created on Day 1 may be harder to recover from than no personalization claim at all; copy and UX framing must be calibrated to signal directional personalization, not complete individualization

Supabase schema migration risk — if the existing PostgreSQL schema does not cleanly support a memory layer additive model, a migration mid-sprint could destroy session continuity for existing users during the critical retention improvement cycle

Research track timing — if competitor teardowns and behavioral economics evidence briefs are not completed before onboarding redesign begins, the redesign will proceed on assumption rather than evidence; research must be bounded to 48 hours maximum per track to prevent analysis paralysis

No A/B infrastructure means sequential rollout only — any onboarding variant must be rolled out to 100% of new users simultaneously, meaning a failed variant cannot be isolated to a cohort; Michael must accept that the first iteration is a learning deployment, not a controlled experiment

Exercise science liability exposure — AI-generated programming without certified guardrails creates injury liability at any user scale; legal validation of current exposure level must be completed before any paid marketing or public launch amplification

Instrumentation privacy compliance — adding session event logging without reviewing GDPR, CCPA, and App Store data collection disclosure requirements creates compliance exposure; atlas must flag minimum viable compliance requirements before event logging ships

experiments.hypothesis: If we execute this workstream, the primary objective should improve: 1. Session event instrumentation — instrument screen-by-screen abandonment tracking across the full Day-1 funnel within the first sprint; this is the prerequisite for every other workstream decision
experiments.user_or_workflow: Reduce Day-1 through Day-7 abandonment from ~75% to below 40% by redesigning the onboarding and first-session experience with persistent AI memory, behavioral retention mechanics, and a personalization loop that delivers perceived value before the user has a reason to quit.
experiments.metric_moved: Day-7 retention rate improves from ~25% baseline to 60% or higher within two iteration cycles
